headline: Turkey's Lake Tuz Rebounds: Thousands of Flamingo Chicks Thrive
meta_description: Turkey's Lake Tuz has recovered dramatically from near-collapse, now hosting around 5,000 flamingo chicks—a powerful conservation success story.
summary_short: Turkey's Lake Tuz, which nearly died five years ago, has rebounded with approximately 5,000 flamingo chicks, showcasing the power of dedicated conservation.
body:
Lake Tuz in central Turkey has experienced a remarkable transformation. Just five years ago, the salt lake had dried to crisis levels, and thousands of flamingo chicks perished. Today, the wetland is teeming with life once again, dotted with approximately 5,000 young flamingos being nurtured by attentive parents.

The turnaround is a direct result of focused conservation efforts that restored water levels and protected nesting grounds. Drone footage captured this June reveals dense clusters of juvenile birds learning to forage and navigate their pink-hued world—a striking testament to what dedicated environmental work can achieve.

For one of the planet's most iconic birds, Lake Tuz has transformed from a symbol of ecological collapse into a beacon of hope. The restoration demonstrates that even ecosystems on the brink of failure can recover when communities commit to protection and restoration. For birdwatchers, conservationists, and Turks who cherish this natural wonder, the return of the flamingos is a cause for genuine celebration.
